channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: England kick-off chaos: What really happened and how storms could delay the game
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The first the English FA knew about a possible six-hour shift to the kick-off time for Mexico vs England in the World Cup last 16 was when Mexican journalists started asking Morgan Rogers and Marcus Rashford how they felt about it, I'm told.

item.title: Hamilton plots Ferrari teamwork to 'topple' Antonelli in British GP
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton hopes he can work together with Ferrari team-mate Charles Leclerc to "topple" Kimi Antonelli at the British Grand Prix.

item.title: EKSG Provides Medical Support To Freed Kidnap Victims
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Ekiti State Governor, Mr Biodun Oyebanji, has directed the management of the Ekiti State University Teaching Hospital (EKSUTH), Ado-Ekiti, to provide comprehensive medical screening and treatment for the victims of the Eda Oniyo abduction who have just regained freedom. The Governor also instruc

item.title: 2027 Elections: “Tinubu Has No Real Challenger, Will Win Easily” — Orji Kalu
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Sen. Orji Uzor Kalu has expressed confidence that President Bola Tinubu will win re-election comfortably, insisting that the opposition is too divided to pose any serious challenge. Speaking at an APC gathering, Kalu said Tinubu currently has “no competition” because the opposition is “in tatters.”

item.title: Ogun Police crack down on 89 motorists, smash robbery gang
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Babaseyi explained that the operation was designed to improve vehicle traceability, strengthen intelligence gathering, support criminal investigations and deny criminal elements the anonymity often exploited in carrying out unlawful activities.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Why it’s a good month to save on streaming, despite the return of ‘Enola Holmes’ to Netflix and ‘Silo’ to Apple

item.title: Verstappen: No point racing in British GP if Red Bull can't fix top-speed issue
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Max Verstappen says there is "no point competing" in Sunday's British Grand Prix if his Red Bull team cannot fix an issue that limited his top speed during qualifying.

Abba announced his decision on Friday whil

item.title: “Nigeria’s Debt Is Moderate, Low Revenue Remains Major Challenge” – World Bank
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The World Bank has said Nigeria’s biggest fiscal challenge is weak revenue mobilisation rather than excessive borrowing, urging the government to increase revenue generation to support sustainable economic growth. Speaking on Channels Television on Friday, World Bank Country Director for Nigeria, Ma

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Britain's Arthur Fery overcame a series of nose bleeds to reach the fourth round of Wimbledon with a stunning five-set victory over Eastbourne champion Zizou Bergs.

item.description: In the blink of an eye, we're down to the final 16 nations left competing for the 2026 World Cup. What do our writers and experts think the field looks like from worst to best of the teams remaining?

item.title: To My Grandfather, Dr. Mohammed Abba
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Inna Lillahi Wa Inna Ilaihi Raji’un. My heart is broken. I have tried to find the right words, but I do not know if words can ever portray the journey of a man who was many things to many people. To many, he was a headmaster. To others, he was their lecturer. To some, he […]

item.title: Sources: France's Tchouaméni out vs. Paraguay
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Aurélien Tchouaméni, who left Friday's training with adductor pain, will miss France's World Cup round-of-16 match Saturday vs. Paraguay in Philadelphia, and he could also miss a potential quarterfinal on July 9 should France advance, sources told ESPN.

item.title: Messi: Argentina have 'many' things to correct
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lionel Messi said Argentina have "many" things to improve after a nail-biting 3-2 win over Cape Verde in extra time Friday night at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens, Florida, pointing out his team's shortcomings in the round-of-32 victory.

item.title: Sources: Mexico vs. England to KO six hours early
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Mexico's World Cup round-of-16 clash against England will kick off at 6 p.m. local time, as originally scheduled, in Mexico City on Sunday, sources have told ESPN, after lengthy talks between FIFA and the two national associations Friday.

item.title: Nagelsmann out as Germany boss, Klopp lined up
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp said Friday he has had discussions with the German federation about taking over as national team coach after Julian Nagelsmann's exit.

item.title: Ancelotti: SAF is only manager more qualified
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Brazil manager Carlo Ancelotti had a reply to social media doubters in Brazil on Friday, saying legendary former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson is the only manager more qualified than he is.

item.title: Argentina break Cape Verde hearts in extra-time
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina were taken into extra time by Cape Verde but eventually prevailed 3-2, with Lionel Messi on the scoresheet, in the round of 32 in Miami on Friday.

item.title: Rodman marks NWSL return with dramatic double
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Trinity Rodman scored two goals, including a stoppage-time winner, to lift the Washington Spirit to a 2-1 win over the Houston Dash on Friday..

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: USMNT forward Folarin Balogun said that he's been on an emotional "roller coaster" in the wake of his sending off against Bosnia-Herzegovina on Wednesday, but he intends to "support the boys" as the team prepares to play Belgium in the round of 16.

item.title: Insurers shifted roof-replacement costs onto homeowners thanks to a new federal rule — just in time for hail and hurricane season
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Those who find extensive damage to their roofs might be stuck between two options: File an insurance claim, which could trigger a premium increase, or cough up the cash for the necessary repairs or replacement.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The visit would mark Netanyahu's seventh visit to the US since Trump returned to office for a second term as president.

item.description: Expressing sympathy for victims of the attacks, Maharaj Ji urged African governments to provide assistance to affected citizens and businesses while advocating policies that promote peace, inclusion and mutual respect across the continent.

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jacob Bethell fired an unbeaten 76 off 46 balls as England beat India by four wickets in the second T20I at Old Trafford, spoiling Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's international debut as the 15-year-old made history in Manchester.

item.title: England within touching distance of Lionesses and Red Roses moment
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England's cricketers are a victory away from clinching their own Lionesses and Red Roses moment.

item.title: 5 Most Memorable Super Eagles Matches At The FIFA World Cup
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The Super Eagles may be missing from the ongoing FIFA World Cup 2026, but Nigeria’s rich World Cup history continues to command global respect. Across six appearances at football’s biggest tournament, the three-time African champions have produced unforgettable moments that remain etched

item.title: Where can I invest my kid’s ‘Trump account’ money? The Treasury Department just answered that question.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The money in “Trump accounts” has to be invested in low-cost index funds — and now parents and investors are learning which funds they can actually use.

item.title: World Cup Qualifiers: Nigeria’s D’Tigers face hotel eviction over Unpaid Bills in Angola
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: This is not the first time funding issues have affected the team, as similar challenges reportedly disrupted preparations for previous competitions.

item.title: Jelly Roll’s ex-wife Bunnie Xo says he gave her their $6 million Tennessee estate in divorce
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Country singer Jelly Roll’s ex-wife, Bunnie Xo, has revealed that she will take full ownership of the former couple’s Tennessee compound as part of their divorce settlement.

item.title: Brazil’s Durigan Says Credit Lines Won’t Affect Monetary Policy
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Credit measures introduced by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva’s government do not undermine monetary policy, Finance Minister Dario Durigan said in an interview with local news website G1 published on Saturday.

item.title: World Bank confirms Anambra did not apply for HOPE-Governance programme
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: World Bank confirms Anambra State did not apply for the HOPE-Governance Programme, countering reports of disqualification over benchmarks. The post World Bank confirms Anambra did not apply for HOPE-Governance programme appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Verizon is About to Break our Watches
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.jefftk.com/p/verizon-is-about-to-break-our-watches Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48787329 Points: 26 # Comments: 6

item.title: France vs Paraguay LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 last 16
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Live coverage and text commentary stream updates as Mbappe's France face Paraguay in the Round of 16 knockout game.

item.title: Copy of Cape Verde's incredible World Cup run captured hea...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: If World Cups were measured in moments, then Cape Verde would be champions, as their incredible run to the knockouts captured the hearts of fans.

item.title: Borno: FG flags off Dikwa-Ngala, Bama-Banki road projects
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Government on Saturday reaffirmed its commitment to rebuilding critical infrastructure across the North-East as it flagged off the reconstruction of the Dikwa-Ngala and Bama-Banki roads in Borno State, describing the projects as strategic investments aimed at improving security, stimulat
